title: Grit Studio Run Lifecycle Observation Boundary
forbids: Studio Run in Game runtime observation bypassing the observation port, canonical lifecycle evidence, the shared in-process Studio live reader, or private deployment/correlation evidence boundary
why: Runtime observation is one private workflow boundary: after the package-owned lifecycle start and fresh log proof, the workflow asks the host observation port to correlate deployment, canonical lifecycle setup evidence, scripting-log proof, and loaded-game readback. The app observer reads loaded-game state through a narrow reader over the exact Studio router and shared runtime rather than sending HTTP to its own server.
remediate: Keep observeRunInGameRuntime as the workflow observation port, pass prepared/deployment/started/log inputs, derive setup-row evidence from started.evidence.setup, read loaded-game status and snapshot through the shared in-process Studio live reader, and store observation evidence only in private runtime diagnostics.
message: Studio Run in Game runtime observation boundary drifted.
